Q: What is the solution to this math problem: In a certain year, the 100th day of the year was a Wednesday. Which day of the week was the 100th day of the next year?

A: There are 365 days in a year. Therefore, The 100th day of the next year is 368 days from the 100th day of the first year. Since there are 7 days in a week, 368/7 = 52 with a remainder of 4. 100th day in the first year is Wednesday. Therefore, the 100th day in the next year is 4 days after Wednesday. Hence, the 100th day in the next year is Sunday.
